claimText: The Genetic Engineering Appraisal Committee (GEAC) is a statutory committee constituted under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
evidenceExcerpt: GEAC is a statutory committee under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
examUse: Statement-based question on the legal backing of environmental and biotechnological regulatory bodies.
distractorHooks: Constituted under the Biological Diversity Act, 2002
Constituted under the Wildlife Protection Act, 1972
It is an extra-constitutional or executive body

claimText: GEAC functions under the administrative control of the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEF&CC).
evidenceExcerpt: It functions under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEF&CC).
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying the nodal ministry for GM crop regulation.
distractorHooks: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare

claimText: GEAC is responsible for appraising proposals for the release of genetically engineered organisms and products.
evidenceExcerpt: It appraises proposals for the release of GE organisms and products.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the specific mandate of GEAC regarding GM organisms.
distractorHooks: Appraises only GM food crops and not other GE organisms
Mandate is limited to research and development, not environmental release

claimText: GEAC is mandated to appraise activities involving the large-scale use of hazardous microorganisms and recombinants.
evidenceExcerpt: It appraises large-scale use of hazardous microorganisms and recombinants.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the broader regulatory scope of GEAC beyond GM crops.
distractorHooks: Hazardous microorganisms are exclusively regulated by the Ministry of Health
GEAC only deals with plant genetics, not microorganisms
